title: AI Builders Digest
subtitle: Bilingual edition · 双语对照版

intro.text: 今天这批内容有一条特别清晰的主线：AI 时代一个人的杠杆率正在变得离谱。TRQ 转发了 Anthropic 同事 Suzanne 的一个 prompt——一段话让 Claude 在长任务里自己跟自己确认、有节奏地报告进度，6183 赞，是今天最强的方法论信号。Steipete 让 Codex 卡住时主动语音呼叫他，Thibault 直接说「你直接 Codex 一整个农场」。Dan Shipper 的版本更夸张：「不用一周干七天，只要让一群 Codex 一直在 /goal 跑着就行。」把这些放在一起看，所谓 10x 工程师的故事已经过时了，现在是 100x single-person business 的早期迹象。配套的故事是 Rauchg 宣布 MiniMax M3 已经是 Next.js agent 评测里最强的开源模型，10x 便宜。模型、prompt、组织方式三个变量同时在变，杠杆率的复利已经开始。

archive.title: 单人杠杆日报
archive.desc: Anthropic 的 Suzanne 给出 Claude 长任务节奏 prompt，Codex 开始会主动语音呼叫主人，MiniMax M3 在 Next.js 评测上 10x 便宜——单人杠杆率的复利已经启动。

footerNote: Source: Follow Builders curated daily digest. Rebuilt from the 2026-05-29 source feeds.

sections.title: The Claude Loop Prompt / 让 Claude 跑长任务的节奏 prompt
sections.desc: TRQ 转发的这条 prompt 是今天全网最高赞的方法论分享——它解决了 Claude Code 长任务最容易翻车的问题。

rewrite: TRQ polled other Anthropic folks on how they stay in the loop with Claude and fully understand the work being done. The winning answer came from Suzanne: a single prompt that asks Claude to be honest, give short progress updates, and surface assumptions or risks at each step.

The mechanism is interesting: instead of trying to monitor the whole task, the agent narrates its work in small chunks. The user gets a steady stream of checkpoints they can actually scan, rather than a wall of text at the end.
6183 likes makes this the most resonant method-share of the day. Suzanne also mentioned she uses this together with voice mode so the agent can interrupt her naturally when it needs input.
original: Been asking others at Anthropic how they stay in the loop with Claude and fully understand the work being done. This is one of my favorites from Suzanne.
gist for the full prompt here: https://t.co/L0ffBeU1ua
Suzanne also mentioned she uses this with voice mode to make it easier to respond and more natural.

rewrite: TRQ 问了 Anthropic 内部的同事：你们是怎么和 Claude 保持同步、并且完整理解它正在做的工作的？回答里他最喜欢的是 Suzanne 写的那段 prompt——一句话让 Claude 保持诚实、用短进度更新、在每一步把假设和风险说出来。
机制很有意思：不是试图监控整个任务，而是让 agent 用小段落把工作讲出来。用户拿到的是一段一段可以快速扫的 checkpoint，而不是任务结束后一整面墙的总结。
6183 个赞，是今天方法论分享里共鸣最强的一条。Suzanne 补充说，她还把这段 prompt 和 voice mode 配合用，这样 agent 在需要输入的时候能自然地打断她。
original: TRQ 转发 Suzanne 的 prompt，让 Claude 短更新、暴露假设和风险。
6183 赞。Suzanne 还配合 voice mode 用，让 agent 能自然打断她。

sections.title: Codex as a Coworker / Codex 开始像一个真同事
sections.desc: Codex 越来越像一个会主动找你的同事——卡住了会语音呼叫、可以一打一打地开。

rewrite: Peter Steinberger told Codex to use a voice service whenever it gets stuck and needs the user to unblock it. Now occasionally the agent just talks to him out of nowhere, asking for help.

His example: Codex pinged him during a long review run because it needed access to a repo. He calls it the coolest thing ever—the agent is no longer a tool you check on, it's a colleague that interrupts you.
683 likes confirms this is a real shift in how people relate to coding agents: the boundary between background process and teammate is dissolving.
original: I told codex to use https://t.co/oHS8ombQcW whenever I'm distracted and it needs my help to be unblocked, and ever once it a while I hear it talking to me, and it's the coolest thing ever. (e.g. for review runs and it needs access to a repo)

rewrite: Peter Steinberger 给 Codex 设了一条规则：只要卡住、需要人来解锁，就用语音服务找他。结果偶尔他就听见 agent 自己开口说话，请他帮忙。
他的例子是：一次很长的 review 跑到一半，Codex 因为需要某个 repo 的权限，主动用语音呼叫他。他说这是他见过最酷的事——agent 不再是一个要你主动去查的工具，而是一个会打断你的同事。
683 个赞，说明这不是个例：coding agent 正在从「后台进程」变成「会找你的同事」。
original: Steipete 让 Codex 卡住时用语音服务呼叫他。他说这是最酷的事。

rewrite: Dan Shipper's framing is the most quotable line of the day: you don't have to work 7 days a week if you just have a swarm of Codex's running on /goal all the time. But you'll probably want to.

The point is not that work expands to fill the time available. The point is that with enough agent parallelism, you can always be shipping at the edge of what is possible—which means the natural ceiling stops being the agent count and starts being the founder's appetite.
Shipper says the 'it happened' moment is when he saw the swarm in motion doing real work.
original: you don't have to work 7 days a week if you just have a swarm of Codex's running on /goal all the time. But you'll probably want to
it happened!

rewrite: Dan Shipper 给出了今天最值得引用的金句：你不用一周干七天，只要让一群 Codex 一直在 /goal 跑着就行。不过你八成还是想干。
重点不是说工作会填满时间。重点是当 agent 并行度足够高，你就能一直贴着可能性边界在出货——自然的天花板不再取决于 agent 数量，而取决于创始人自己的胃口。
Shipper 说「it happened」的那一刻，就是他看到整群 agent 真的在动起来干活。
original: Dan Shipper：一群 Codex 在 /goal 跑着，你不用一周干七天。
不过你八成还是想干。it happened。

rewrite: Thibault Sottiaux at OpenAI drops the pithiest one-liner of the day: 'You can just codex ... a farm.' The joke collapses what used to be a serious engineering project—provisioning, monitoring, scaling—into a verb the agent can do for you.
Combined with his 2984-like gag about renaming Codex to ChadGPT, the message is that Codex is no longer being treated as a tool, it's being treated as infrastructure you deploy.
original: You can just codex ... a farm
Should we rename Codex to ChadGPT?

rewrite: OpenAI 的 Thibault Sottiaux 抛出了今天最精炼的一句话：「You can just codex ... a farm」。这个玩笑把过去要认真做的工程项目——provisioning、监控、扩容——压成了一个 agent 可以替你做的动词。
再配合他那条 2984 赞的「要不要把 Codex 改名 ChadGPT」，信号很清楚：Codex 不再被当成一个工具，而是被当成你可以部署的基础设施。
original: Thibault：You can just codex ... a farm。
Codex 已经是基础设施，不是工具。

sections.title: The Open Model Frontier / 开源模型的前沿又往前推了一格
sections.desc: Rauchg 宣布 MiniMax M3 在 Next.js agent 评测上拿到开源第一，10x 便宜，这是 2026 年开源模型叙事的关键节点之一。

rewrite: Guillermo Rauch announces that MiniMax M3 is now the leading open model on Next.js agent evaluations. It sits right behind Opus and GPT-5, but is 10x cheaper—and 20x cheaper on Vercel's AI Gateway this week.

The takeaway is structural: open models are no longer 'good enough', they are now winning on price-performance in production agent benchmarks. Closed-model labs lose pricing power precisely where agents are most cost-sensitive.
596 likes, plus 25 retweets—the developer audience is treating this as a meaningful threshold crossed, not a benchmark curiosity.
original: MiniMax M3 is now the leading open model on the Next.js agent evaluations (https://t.co/SnZ54XoRWV).
Right behind Opus & GPT5, but 10× cheaper (And 20× cheaper right now on ▲ AI Gateway!)

rewrite: Guillermo Rauch 宣布 MiniMax M3 已经成为 Next.js agent 评测上的开源第一。性能紧贴 Opus 和 GPT-5，但价格便宜 10x——而且本周在 Vercel 自家的 AI Gateway 上还便宜到 20x。
结构性的结论是：开源模型已经不止是「够用」，它们开始在生产级 agent 评测里赢性价比。闭源模型在 agent 最敏感的成本维度上正在丧失定价权。
596 个赞加 25 次转发——开发者群体把这当成一个被跨过的门槛，而不是一个 benchmark 趣闻。
original: Rauchg：MiniMax M3 成为 Next.js agent 评测上的开源第一，10x 便宜。
596 赞。开源模型在生产 agent 评测上开始赢性价比。

sections.title: Competitive Advantage in the Agent Era / Agent 时代的护城河
sections.desc: 当每个对手都能用上同样的模型时，企业怎么建立差异？Levie 和 Garry Tan 从不同角度给出了答案。

rewrite: Aaron Levie poses the defining question of the era: when your competitor has access to the same AI models and intelligence, how do you build a durable advantage?

His answer, which he has been building toward for weeks: the moat is not the model, it is the proprietary workflow, the customer relationship, the data flywheel, the integration depth. Agents are commoditized; the context in which they run is not.
The implication for builders: the company that wins is not the one with the best agent, it's the one that wires the agent most tightly into the real operations of a real customer.
original: As we enter the era of AI agents, one of the defining questions is how you develop competitive advantage when your competitor has access to the same AI models and intelligence as you.

rewrite: Aaron Levie 抛出了这个时代的核心问题：当你的对手也能用上同样的 AI 模型和智能时，你怎么建立持续的优势？
他的答案是他最近几周一直在搭的那个结论：护城河不是模型本身，而是专有的工作流、客户关系、数据飞轮、集成的深度。Agent 会变成商品，能跑 Agent 的 context 不会。
对 builder 的含义是：赢的那家公司不是拥有最好 agent 的那家，而是把 agent 缝进真实客户真实运营里缝得最紧的那家。
original: Levie：Agent 时代护城河不是模型，是专有 workflow、数据飞轮和客户集成深度。
Agent 会商品化，context 不会。

rewrite: Levie reads the AWS-OpenAI partnership as a distribution play on both sides: AWS has massive enterprise traction with large committed contracts, so this opens up increased distribution for OpenAI's models. It also likely drives an increase in inference capacity and compute availability for OpenAI.
His framing: the agent era is pushing the big infra players into new combinations. Whoever controls the enterprise distribution will increasingly shape whose models customers actually run.
original: AWS has massive enterprise traction, with large committed contracts from enterprises. So this partnership opens up both increased distribution for OpenAI's models, but also likely drives an increase in inference capacity and compute availability for OpenAI.

rewrite: Levie 把 AWS-OpenAI 的合作解读成双向的 distribution 动作：AWS 在企业里根基深、合约大，所以这次合作一方面给 OpenAI 的模型打开了企业渠道，另一方面也给 OpenAI 带来了更多推理容量和算力。
他的框架是：Agent 时代正在把大的基础设施玩家推向新的组合。谁掌控企业渠道，谁就越来越能决定客户的模型到底跑在谁家。
original: Levie：AWS-OpenAI 合作是企业渠道 + 推理容量的双向 distribution 动作。

sections.title: Demis Hassabis: Race, Regret, and Comeback / Demis 谈竞赛、后悔与回归
sections.desc: Sebastian Mallaby 给 Demis 写完传记后第一次完整访谈，关键信息是：竞赛是必然的，Demis 已经从单点安全转向集体行动问题，而 Google DeepMind 的回归比外界以为的早。

cards.authorTag: Redpoint AI 出品的 AI 行业访谈播客

rewrite: Biographer Sebastian Mallaby sat down with Demis Hassabis for over thirty hours for his new book The Infinity Machine. The strongest frame from the interview: the AI race was inevitable. When the technology is this strong, multiple labs in multiple countries are going to try to build it, full stop.

Demis's view has flipped: he started hoping for a singleton scenario—essentially DeepMind alone. He now believes safety is a collective action problem that only governments can solve. One safe lab doesn't make the world safer if the others aren't.

On the Google DeepMind comeback: people were too quick to crown OpenAI as the winner. As of late 2025, Gemini 3.0 was ahead on the leaderboards, before the recent Anthropic surge. The 'Google is too slow' narrative has aged badly.
original: I think when you have this sort of supremely strong technology, there's gonna be multiple labs in multiple countries that are just desperate to try and build it.
He has swung from one extreme to the other. He began by thinking there could be a singleton scenario, just one lab. To the opposite extreme where he now sees that there's a very crowded field.
People were too quick to crown OpenAI and Sam Altman as the winner and underestimated both Demis as a person and Google DeepMind as a company.

rewrite: 传记作者 Sebastian Mallaby 为了写《The Infinity Machine》跟 Demis 坐下来谈了三四十个小时。访谈里最有力的一条框架是：AI 竞赛是必然的。当技术这么强的时候，多个国家、多个实验室一定会去争相建造它，没有第二种可能。
Demis 的看法已经翻转了：他最初希望出现一个 singleton 的局面——基本上就是 DeepMind 自己。现在他认为安全是一个只有政府才能解决的集体行动问题。一个实验室安全没用，如果其他的不安全。
关于 Google DeepMind 的回归：外界太早给 OpenAI 加冕了。到 2025 年底，Gemini 3.0 在 leaderboard 上其实是领先的，Anthropic 最近的崛起是后面的事。「Google 太慢」的叙事已经严重过期。
original: Demis：AI 竞赛是必然的，多个实验室多个国家一定会去争。
从 singleton 转向集体行动问题：安全只能由政府来解。
Google DeepMind 的回归比外界以为的早：2025 年底 Gemini 3.0 在 leaderboard 上其实领先。

rewrite: Sam Altman flags the OpenAI Foundation as a separate workstream: the foundation is doing a lot of wonderful things, and helping society become resilient to AI is going to be incredibly important. Much more to come.

The signal is positioning: while competitors argue about model races, the OpenAI side is also building a public-facing story about societal resilience. The foundation becomes a soft-power asset in the regulatory conversation that Mallaby describes.
1661 likes and 129 retweets—read as a fundraising/legitimacy play, not a product announcement.
original: The OpenAI Foundation is doing a lot of wonderful things.
Helping society become resilient to AI is going to be incredibly important. Much more to come here!

rewrite: Sam Altman 把 OpenAI 基金会单拎出来讲：基金会做了很多了不起的事，帮助社会对 AI 产生韧性这件事会极其重要，更多内容以后会公布。
信号是定位：当对手还在争模型竞赛时，OpenAI 这边在同时搭建一个面向公众的「社会韧性」叙事。基金会在 Mallaby 描述的那种监管对话里会变成一种软实力资产。
1661 个赞加 129 次转发——按融资和合法性叙事来读，不是产品发布。
original: Sam Altman 强调 OpenAI 基金会在做「让社会对 AI 有韧性」的工作，更多内容以后公布。

sections.title: Solo Building with Agents / 单兵作战的能力边界在快速外推
sections.desc: Peter Yang 整理的 Josh Shpigford 经验 + Amasad 的 prompt-to-business——单个人或极小团队能做的生意种类在肉眼可见地扩大。

rewrite: Peter Yang's top-6 takeaways from Josh Shpigford distill 25 years of solo-building experience into AI-agent-era advice. The standout rule: keep shipping even if the fear of embarrassment never goes away. Shpigford has been shipping solo for decades, and the embarrassment didn't go away—he just stopped letting it stop him.

The other lessons tighten around a clear thesis: build multiple small products with AI agents, treat the agents as your team, and pick problems that have a real dollar behind them rather than vanity distribution. The 2025 version of solo building is structurally different from the indie hacker era—the agents let you cover surface area that used to need three or four people.
original: My top 6 takeaways from @Shpigford on how to build multiple products solo with AI agents: 1. Keep shipping even if the fear of embarrassment never goes away. After 25 years as a solo builder, Josh s...

rewrite: Peter Yang 整理的 Josh Shpigford 六条心得把 25 年单兵作战的经验压缩成 AI agent 时代的忠告。最出挑的一条是：持续出货，即使尴尬的恐惧从来不会消失。Shpigford 单干了二十多年，那种尴尬感并没有消失，他只是不再让它拦住自己。
其他几条汇成一个清楚的论点：用 AI agent 同时做多个小产品，把 agent 当团队用，去解决背后有真实美元的问题，而不是去做流量好看的项目。2025 年的单兵作战和 indie hacker 时代结构上不一样了——agent 让你能覆盖的表面积是以前要三四个人才能撑住的。
original: Peter Yang 整理 Josh Shpigford 单干 25 年的六条经验：第一条是持续出货，即使尴尬感没消失。
AI agent 时代单人能覆盖的表面积是以前 3-4 个人的活。

rewrite: Amjad Masad's pitch is the cleanest version of the 'agents as a full team' argument: from one prompt, you get a website, a mobile app, monetization wired in, and a Delaware corp to operate from. Free to start.

Strip away the marketing and the underlying claim is that the fixed costs of starting a software business—setup, app shells, payment integration, legal structure—have collapsed to near zero. That changes who can compete and how fast.

167 likes and 15 retweets is modest for Amjad, but the message is the product positioning the company wants out there: a one-prompt-to-business story is exactly the narrative this market wants to believe is true.
original: Prompt to business: - website - mobile app - monetization - Delaware corp. Start for free

rewrite: Amjad Masad 抛出了「agent 就是完整团队」最干净的版本：从一段 prompt 出发，你得到一个网站、一个移动 app、内嵌的变现通道，以及一家可以开张运营的特拉华州公司。免费起步。
把营销话术剥掉，底下的论断是：开一家软件公司的固定成本——搭架子、App 壳、支付集成、法律结构——已经压到了接近零。这改变了谁能参与竞争以及竞争的速度。
167 赞和 15 次转发对 Amjad 来说不算爆，但信息正是公司想推出去的产品定位：一段 prompt 到一家公司，恰好是这个市场最愿意相信的叙事。
original: Amjad：prompt → 网站 + 移动 app + 变现 + 特拉华公司。免费起步。
软件创业的固定成本已经压到接近零。

sections.title: Operators in Public / 运营者公开他们的 workflow
sections.desc: Matt Turck、Garry Tan、Nikunj、Aditya 等都在主动展示自己怎么用 agent、怎么构建公司——公开化本身就是一种信号。

rewrite: Matt Turck shares a viral CEO-vs-CTO meme: the CEO says 'we have tens of thousands of AI agents running in production at massive scale right now', and the CTO is rendered as a small, defeated-looking figure with no comeback.
Read as truth, it captures the awkward gap between executive narrative and engineering reality at every company currently doing a public AI push. Read as comedy, it lands because the gap is real.
289 likes, 12 retweets. The point that survives the joke: 'tens of thousands of agents in production' is a phrase that has lost all information value—everyone says it, no one defines it.
original: CEO: 'we have tens of thousands of AI agents running in production at massive scale right now'. CTO:

rewrite: Matt Turck 转了一个爆火的 CEO vs CTO meme：CEO 说「我们现在在生产环境里有几万个 AI agent 跑着，量级巨大」，CTO 被打成一个小小的、表情委屈的小人，毫无还嘴之力。
当事实读，它抓住了每家正在公开讲 AI 的公司里那种「高管叙事 vs 工程现实」的尴尬鸿沟。当笑话读，它能炸也是因为这道鸿沟是真实的。
289 个赞，12 次转发。玩笑背后留下的判断是：「生产环境里几万个 agent」这句话已经失去信息量——谁都在说，谁都没定义清楚。
original: Matt Turck 转 CEO vs CTO meme：CEO 喊几万个 agent 上生产，CTO 毫无还嘴。
这句话已经失去信息量。

rewrite: Garry Tan puts the GStack /office-hours workflow one click away: a single button to try it on your own product idea. The structural claim: founder-office-hours is now a thing you can rent from an AI.

Garry's stack has been the most-cited YC-region operator workflow of the past few months. The public release of a one-click version is the natural step—expose the exact thing that was previously a Garry-only advantage.
352 likes and 20 retweets. The YC audience is the natural customer for this kind of product, so the launch is also a distribution play disguised as a workflow share.
original: Want to try GStack /office-hours for your product idea as quickly as possible? It's one click here: https://t.co/8g3LJkrV6i

rewrite: Garry Tan 把 GStack 的 /office-hours 工作流做成了一键版：按一个键就可以把它用在你自己的产品 idea 上。底层的论断是：founder-office-hours 现在已经是 AI 可以替你租的活。
Garry 这套工作流是过去几个月 YC 圈里被引用最多的运营者 workflow。一键版的公开发布是自然的下一步——把以前只是 Garry 独占优势的那个东西，开放出来。
352 赞和 20 次转发。YC 受众是这类产品天然的客户，所以这次发布也是一次 workflow 分享外壳下的渠道动作。
original: Garry Tan：GStack /office-hours 一键可用。

rewrite: Nikunj Kothari's one-liner is a market-history punchline: all you had to do to make life-changing wealth was to invest in ~every public company along the 237. The bus route in Silicon Valley. The implied thesis is that the diversification you sneer at is the diversification that compounded.
It's also a meta-joke about how hard it is to pick winners. The 237 buses between Sand Hill and San Francisco were, in retrospect, a 20-year rolling portfolio of every AI company in the world.
4045 likes makes this the most popular pure 'builder humor' tweet of the day.
original: All you had to do to make life changing wealth was to invest in ~every public company along the 237

rewrite: Nikunj Kothari 的一句玩笑是市场史的冷幽默：想要赚到改变人生的财富，你只需要投资 237 沿线每一家上市公司。237 是硅谷的那条公交线。隐含的判断是：你嘴上嫌弃的分散投资，恰恰是真正复利的那一个。
这也是一句关于「赢家难选」的元玩笑。Sand Hill 和旧金山之间跑的 237 公交，事后看是一条二十年滚动组合——把全世界每一家 AI 公司都装进去了。
4045 个赞，让这条成为今天最受欢迎的「builder 幽默」。
original: Nikunj：赚到改变人生的财富，只需要投资 237 沿线每一家上市公司。

rewrite: Aditya Agarwal flags Schrep and the Gigascale team as a partner South Park Commons portfolio companies rave about. The post is short, but the subtext is a talent-flow note: the people who used to build hyperscale infra at Meta are now building for AI-native companies, and SPC is the connective tissue.
17 likes and 1 retweet means the audience is small but the message is directed: if you're a portfolio founder choosing infra partners, here's a curated signal.
original: Schrep and the Gigascale team are building something special. They have been incredible partners to @southpkcommons portfolio companies. @schrep is someone who I have looked up to for a long time...

rewrite: Aditya Agarwal 把 Schrep 和 Gigascale 团队点名推荐为 South Park Commons 投资组合公司反复夸赞的合作伙伴。推文很短，但底下是一条人才流向笔记：以前在 Meta 做超大规模基础设施的人，现在在给 AI 原生公司做基建，SPC 是中间那条线。
